client_context.facility_line: Munich Bread Works & Vienna Pastries, Germany / Austria
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU, German & Austrian Sustainability Frameworks for Bakery Manufacturers

executive_summary:
January 2026 is the deadline month for ARA's first quarterly payment and the commencement of Brösel Backwaren GmbH's most intensive compliance workstream — the ESRS double materiality assessment and LkSG supply chain risk assessment running in parallel. The ARA payment of EUR 4,210 was processed on 28 January 2026, and Vienna Pastries has been assigned a dedicated ARA account manager for the FY2025 quantity declaration process.

Freshfields' preliminary LkSG risk assessment delivered on 22 January 2026 identifies palm oil from Brösel's Singaporean commodity trader (IOI Corporation sourcing) as the highest-risk supply chain node, classified as 'substantial risk' under LkSG §5(2)(2) due to documented land rights cases involving IOI's Malaysian concessions published in the BHRRC database. Freshfields recommends Brösel immediately issue a supplier risk notification under LkSG §9 and seek alternative RSPO-certified supply or a supplier audit within 60 days.

The EU CSRD DMA is underway with KPMG Munich. January scoping sessions confirmed that E1 (climate — natural gas bakery ovens at Munich Bread Works consuming ~42,000 MWh/year) and S2 (workers in value chain — palm oil and cocoa sourcing) are material financial topics. The Munich facility's oven fleet was installed 2009–2014 and operates below current best-available-technology efficiency benchmarks, creating both an operational improvement opportunity and an ESRS E1 disclosure obligation.

sections.heading: Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz
paragraphs: Freshfields preliminary assessment identifies IOI Corporation-sourced Malaysian palm oil as 'substantial risk' under LkSG §5(2)(2) due to documented BHRRC land rights cases. Brösel must issue a supplier risk notification under LkSG §9 and seek RSPO-certified alternative or conduct supplier audit within 60 days (by 21 March 2026).
sections.callout: Issue LkSG §9 supplier risk notification to Singaporean commodity trader by 5 February 2026; identify RSPO-certified alternative palm oil suppliers for Vienna Pastries with minimum 90-day lead time.

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive — DMA Progress
paragraphs: KPMG Munich DMA confirmed E1 (climate) material: Munich Bread Works natural gas consumption of 42,000 MWh/year places it in bottom quartile of EU food manufacturing energy intensity. Oven fleet age (2009-2014) and efficiency gap create both ESRS E1 disclosure obligation and investment case for oven replacement programme. Munich facility's SBTi-aligned reduction target would require 25% energy intensity reduction by 2030.
sections.callout: Commission oven efficiency audit at Munich Bread Works to quantify 2030 reduction pathway; include capex plan in ESRS E1 transition plan narrative.

sections.heading: Verpackungsdurchführungsgesetz Amendment
paragraphs: Packaging LCA commissioned from Fraunhofer IVV (Munich) delivered preliminary recyclability scoring for Munich Bread Works PE/EVOH bread bags. Preliminary classification: Class C (score 38/100) due to EVOH barrier inseparability and PE matrix recyclability at current German sorting infrastructure. Alternative mono-material PE design without EVOH barrier achieves Class B (score 61/100) but reduces shelf life by 4 days — a significant issue for Brösel's supermarket distribution channels.
sections.callout: Engage modified atmosphere packaging specialists to evaluate high-barrier mono-material PE alternatives that could achieve Class B while maintaining 21-day shelf life; decision required by 30 April 2026.
